hooks.command: FILE=$(echo $STDIN_JSON | jq -r '.tool_input.file_path // ""'); CONTENT=$(echo $STDIN_JSON | jq -r '.tool_input.content // ""'); if [[ "$FILE" =~ \.py$ ]] && echo "$CONTENT" | grep -q 'print('; then echo 'Warning: print() statements should be replaced with logging' >&2; exit 2; fi

FILE=$(echo $STDIN_JSON | jq -r '.tool_input.file_path // ""'); if [[ "$FILE" == "pyproject.toml" ]]; then echo 'Checking for vulnerable dependencies...'; if command -v pip-audit >/dev/null 2>&1; then pip-audit; elif command -v safety >/dev/null 2>&1; then safety check; else echo 'No security audit tool found. Install safety or pip-audit'; fi; fi

CMD=$(echo $STDIN_JSON | jq -r '.tool_input.command // ""'); if echo "$CMD" | grep -qE 'git restore [^-]|git restore$'; then echo 'BLOCKED: git restore discards uncommitted changes' >&2; exit 2; fi; if echo "$CMD" | grep -qE 'git checkout -- '; then echo 'BLOCKED: git checkout -- discards uncommitted changes irreversibly' >&2; exit 2; fi; if echo "$CMD" | grep -qE 'git clean -[a-z]*f'; then echo 'BLOCKED: git clean -f deletes untracked files' >&2; exit 2; fi; if echo "$CMD" | grep -qE 'git reset --hard'; then echo 'BLOCKED: git reset --hard discards uncommitted changes' >&2; exit 2; fi; if echo "$CMD" | grep -qE 'git stash drop'; then echo 'BLOCKED: git stash drop requires explicit user confirmation' >&2; exit 2; fi

CMD=$(echo $STDIN_JSON | jq -r '.tool_input.command // ""'); if echo "$CMD" | grep -qE 'gh pr merge'; then echo 'BLOCKED: Merging PRs requires explicit user approval' >&2; exit 2; fi; if echo "$CMD" | grep -qE 'git push[^|]*:(main|staging)'; then echo 'BLOCKED: Pushing to main/staging requires explicit user approval' >&2; exit 2; fi

CMD=$(echo $STDIN_JSON | jq -r '.tool_input.command // ""'); if echo "$CMD" | grep -qE 'git add (-A|--all)( |$)'; then echo 'BLOCKED: git add -A not allowed — specify exact file paths' >&2; exit 2; fi; if echo "$CMD" | grep -qE 'git add \.( |$)'; then echo 'BLOCKED: git add . not allowed — specify exact file paths' >&2; exit 2; fi

CMD=$(echo $STDIN_JSON | jq -r '.tool_input.command // ""'); if echo "$CMD" | grep -qE 'git push.*(--force|--force-with-lease)'; then echo 'BLOCKED: Force push requires explicit user confirmation. Stop and ask.' >&2; exit 2; fi

CMD=$(echo $STDIN_JSON | jq -r '.tool_input.command // ""'); if echo "$CMD" | grep -q 'git commit' && echo "$CMD" | grep -qi 'co-authored-by'; then echo 'BLOCKED: Co-Authored-By trailers are not allowed in commits' >&2; exit 2; fi

CMD=$(echo $STDIN_JSON | jq -r '.tool_input.command // ""'); BYPASS=''; if echo "$CMD" | grep -qE '^uv run pytest |^python -m pytest '; then BYPASS='pytest — use make agent-test'; fi; if echo "$CMD" | grep -qE '^ruff (check|format) '; then BYPASS='ruff — use make agent-lint'; fi; if echo "$CMD" | grep -qE '^(npx )?tsc '; then BYPASS='tsc — use make agent-frontend-typecheck'; fi; if echo "$CMD" | grep -qE '^(npx )?eslint |^pnpm exec eslint '; then BYPASS='eslint — use make agent-frontend-lint'; fi; if [ -n "$BYPASS" ] && [ -f Makefile ]; then echo "Warning: called $BYPASS (Makefile First)" >&2; exit 1; fi
FILE=$(echo $STDIN_JSON | jq -r '.tool_input.file_path // ""'); if [[ "$FILE" =~ \.py$ ]]; then make lint-file FILE="$FILE" 2>/dev/null || true; fi

FILE=$(echo $STDIN_JSON | jq -r '.tool_input.file_path // ""'); if [[ "$FILE" =~ \.py$ ]]; then RESULT=$(make typecheck-file FILE="$FILE" 2>&1); if [ $? -ne 0 ]; then echo "Type check issues: $RESULT" >&2; exit 2; fi; fi

FILE=$(echo $STDIN_JSON | jq -r '.tool_input.file_path // ""'); if [[ "$FILE" =~ \.py$ && "$FILE" != *"test_"* && "$FILE" != *"_test.py" && "$FILE" != *"/tests/"* ]]; then BASENAME=$(basename "$FILE" .py); for TEST_DIR in backend/tests backend/listings/tests backend/users/tests; do TEST_FILE="$TEST_DIR/test_$BASENAME.py"; if [ -f "$TEST_FILE" ]; then echo "Running $TEST_FILE..."; cd backend && uv run python -m pytest "../$TEST_FILE" -v; cd -; break; fi; done; fi

FILE=$(echo $STDIN_JSON | jq -r '.tool_input.file_path // ""'); if [ -z "$FILE" ] || [[ "$FILE" =~ test_ ]] || [[ "$FILE" =~ _test\. ]] || [[ "$FILE" =~ /tests/ ]]; then exit 0; fi; if [ -f "$FILE" ]; then LINES=$(wc -l < "$FILE"); if [ "$LINES" -gt 500 ]; then echo "BLOCKED: $FILE is $LINES lines — hard max is 500. Split before continuing." >&2; exit 2; elif [ "$LINES" -gt 300 ]; then echo "Warning: $FILE is $LINES lines — target is 300. Look for a natural seam to split." >&2; exit 1; fi; fi

CMD=$(echo $STDIN_JSON | jq -r '.tool_input.command // ""'); if echo "$CMD" | grep -qE 'git commit' && ! echo "$CMD" | grep -qE 'amend|--fixup|--squash'; then MSG=$(git log -1 --format='%B' 2>/dev/null); if [ -n "$MSG" ] && ! echo "$MSG" | grep -q 'Co-created with Claude'; then echo 'Warning: commit is missing attribution footer' >&2; exit 1; fi; fi

hooks.prompt:
You are a code quality reviewer. Review the edit that was just made to $ARGUMENTS.

Check ONLY for these specific violations:

1. DRY: Does this duplicate logic that already exists nearby? (Ignore test files)
2. FAIL-FAST: Does this add a try/except that silently swallows errors, or guard checks (if key in dict) where the key should always exist?
3. OBVIOUS COMMENTS: Does this add comments that restate what the code plainly says? (e.g. '# Returns the user' above getUser())
4. YAGNI: Does this add methods, classes, or abstractions not required by the current task?

If you find a clear violation, return: BLOCK: [principle]: [one-line description]
If no violations or only minor style issues, return: APPROVE

Be conservative — only flag clear, unambiguous violations. When in doubt, APPROVE.

hooks.command: CHANGED=$(git diff --name-only --diff-filter=ACMR 2>/dev/null | grep '\.py$'); if [[ -n "$CHANGED" ]]; then echo 'Linting changed Python files...'; make agent-lint 2>&1 || true; fi
CHANGED=$(git diff --name-only --diff-filter=ACMR 2>/dev/null | grep '\.py$'); if [[ -n "$CHANGED" ]]; then echo 'Type checking changed files...'; make agent-typecheck 2>&1 || true; fi
